evidence: V and Sam bonded over the concept of "flow" and the necessity of having just enough challenge to foster growth, reflecting on McKinsey experiences.
McKinsey/QuantumBlack experienced undisciplined growth post-COVID, leading to morale drop when work didn't match hiring scale.
Jason highlighted a major shift in NYC towards "Vibe Coding"—where non-technical people build functional products using AI tools like Windsurf and Cursor faster than traditional dev cycles.
"Vibe Coding" excels at rapid prototyping and testing user experience, reducing reliance on expensive dev agencies; bridges technical/non-technical gap.
Jason runs a newsletter curating tech professionals (OpenAI, Apple, Meta) for unpaid speaker/judge roles at events.
